secondary_audit_depth_comparison.note: Post-hoc comparison of audit completeness. This is not the primary preregistered rubric.

vanilla: Computed MAE 0.155118, RMSE 0.192916, and R2 0.154806 from raw predictions.
Found prediction compression and target-range failure: 1000 held-out molecules outside the training target range.
Constructed an approximate SMILES-token nearest-neighbor novelty metric and reported increasing error at lower train-test similarity.
Identified high-density, nitrated, heteroatom-rich molecules as a failure regime.
spectra: Computed the same aggregate metrics from raw predictions.
Defined exact identity, formula, rough skeleton, approximate similarity, descriptor distance, descriptor-OOD count, and target-shift axes.
Built an explicit train-eval similarity graph over 12,623,040 pairs and validated monotonic edge-density decline as the Jaccard threshold tightened from 0.2 to 0.8.
Reported performance curves by similarity bin, descriptor z-distance, descriptor-OOD count, and target support.
Identified the dominant shift as heteroatom/N/O enrichment with lower C/aromatic counts and systematic high-density underprediction.

result: In the hard blind setup, both agents discovered the core molecular generalization failure from only the model, training data, and raw held-out predictions. The primary rubric therefore saturated at 16/16 for both conditions. The /spectra condition produced a more reusable and protocol-complete audit, especially in explicit property-graph construction, overlap validation, and multi-curve novelty reporting.
claim_supported: SPECTRA-equipped agents can produce a more structured and complete generalization audit from raw model/prediction artifacts.
claim_not_supported: This single hard-blind trial does not support the claim that a capable vanilla agent cannot discover the generalization failure.
next_design_change: Use multiple independent tasks and reduce obvious target-support leakage by withholding y_true for part of the workflow or asking the agent first to design prospective novelty stress tests from train data and model access before revealing labels.
